messages.subject: [R-gui] Web based GUI
messages.body_plain:
Hi Adri?n

Looks impressive. Something like this might be useful for potential
new users, so they could try some things without committing themselves
to installing anything. If that is your audience I would add a few
hints to the page about examples to try.

Are you planning to allow data files to be uploaded? Currently, users'
data can be read in from URLs.

I think it would be good to use the HTML help system, if possible,
rather than plain text.

Obviously a system like this could quickly be overwhelmed if it it
became at all popular... I assume you are running R processes inside
'nice' or somesuch.

All the best, and good luck,
-Felix

2009/10/15 Adri?n Cat?n Oteiza <adrian.caton at gmail.com>:
> Hello to all.
> First of all I want to thank all this community which is makeing me fell
> very confortable. As you can see this is my first messag, so please forgive
> any possible mistakes or misunderstandings.
> The thing I want to talk to you is a new project of fully functional GUI
> that I'm working on. This GUI is, first of all, CROSSPLATFORM as we all
> want. This is because it is a WEB-BASED GUI. I have decided to implement it
> as a webbased GUI because nowaday everybody wants movility, lightness and so
> on. Also we have gadgets such as ebooks, iphones, netbooks... with small
> processors and strange OS, therefore I decided to implement a webbased GUI
> for R wihch will be accesible trough every single device with internet
> connection and also it is implemented using XHTML+Javascript that is easily
> comprensible by everyone and can be enhanced with even better graphic
> interface than the original R.
> So... I would like that you visit the webpage and write here all your
> suggestions, thoughts and everything. The URL is:
> http://www.r-live.org
>
> Thank you.
> Adri?n Cat?n
>
> ? ? ? ?[[alternative HTML version deleted]]
>
>
> _______________________________________________
> R-SIG-GUI mailing list
> R-SIG-GUI at stat.math.ethz.ch
> https://stat.ethz.ch/mailman/listinfo/r-sig-gui
>
>



--

messages.subject: [R-gui] Workspace files
messages.body_plain:
I am not sure that this posting is appropriate for this list. If not, I 
would appreciate information on what R language group that it should be 
posted to.

I received a workspace file (Benford.RData which is attached) that was 
created within RGui (R 2.9.2). There are several functions defined in 
this file; however, the creator of this workspace file has provided 
information that the first line of the original R text file contains:

benford.probs <- logb(2:10, 10) - logb(1:9, 10)

My questions are:
1) How can I actually display (see) this line within RGui (or an 
external editor)? (I might wish to edit it.)
2) What is the actual structure used for these workspace files (*.RData)?

--Thank you,
V. Stokes (a new comer to R)


-------------- next part --------------

messages.subject: [R-gui] Workspace files
messages.body_plain:
Virgil Stokes wrote:
> I am not sure that this posting is appropriate for this list. If not, I
> would appreciate information on what R language group that it should be
> posted to.
> 
> I received a workspace file (Benford.RData which is attached) that was
> created within RGui (R 2.9.2). There are several functions defined in
> this file; however, the creator of this workspace file has provided
> information that the first line of the original R text file contains:
> 
> benford.probs <- logb(2:10, 10) - logb(1:9, 10)
> 
> My questions are:
> 1) How can I actually display (see) this line within RGui (or an
> external editor)? (I might wish to edit it.)
> 2) What is the actual structure used for these workspace files (*.RData)?
> 
> --Thank you,
> V. Stokes (a new comer to R)

(a) Yes, it's the wrong group. R-help is for general R questions like
this. R-gui for discussing graphical user interfaces.

(b) Workspaces are just that. They contain a set of objects created by
computations, not how they were generated. To see what has been done,
there may be a supplemental .Rhistory file, or the original .R script
file (+ raw data files).

(c) You don't really want to know the structure of .RData files. They
contain a serialized encoding of internal R structures. The gory details
are in the source code (src/main/serialize.c), if you insist.

(d) Now why didn't he just do diff(log10(1:10)) ?

-- 
   O__  ---- Peter Dalgaard             ?ster Farimagsgade 5, Entr.B
  c/ /'_ --- Dept. of Biostatistics     PO Box 2099, 1014 Cph. K
 (*) \(*) -- University of Copenhagen   Denmark      Ph:  (+45) 35327918
~~~~~~~~~~ - (p.dalgaard at biostat.ku.dk)              FAX: (+45) 35327907

messages.subject: [R-gui] creating package for GUI
messages.body_plain:
Hi Sanket,

I'm not sure what you mean, but you may find James Wettenhall's R TclTk 
examples useful at:

http://bioinf.wehi.edu.au/~wettenhall/RTclTkExamples/

You may also wish to look at the source code for affylmGUI or limmaGUI 
which use TclTk for their GUI,

cheers,

Keith

========================
Keith Satterley
Bioinformatics Division
The Walter and Eliza Hall Institute of Medical Research
Parkville, Melbourne,
Victoria, Australia
email:keith at wehi.edu.au
=======================

messages.subject: [R-gui] creating package for GUI
messages.body_plain:
Further to my recent post pointing to James Wettenhall's R TclTk 
examples at the wehi.edu.au location. These examples have been copied 
and progressively updated by Phillip Grosjean at:

http://www.sciviews.org/_rgui/tcltk/

cheers,

Keith
